17692611.summary: Pershing Square Holdings purchased 37,378 public shares on 16 July 2026 through Jefferies at an average price of $52.91 USD (3,921 pence). The buyback comprised 25,713 shares on LSE (PSH ticker) and 11,665 shares (PSHD ticker). The purchase price sits below the calculated NAV of $77.06 per share as of 14 July. Following the transaction, 173.8 million public shares remain outstanding, with 37.1 million shares held in treasury.
17692611.brief: PSH repurchased 37,378 shares at average $52.91 on 16 July 2026.
17692611.take: Share repurchase at a discount to NAV is modestly accretive to remaining shareholders. Routine capital management activity; signals management confidence but represents minimal market-moving catalyst.

17692616.summary: Societe Generale notified on 16 July 2026 of a major holding acquisition in Intermediate Capital Group PLC, increasing its voting rights from 4.46% to 6.34%. The position comprises 17.68m shares (6.26% direct voting rights) plus 228,528 voting rights through cash-settled contracts for difference expiring between December 2027 and May 2029. Total voting rights held: 17.9m shares. This represents a ~190 basis point increase from the previous notification threshold.
17692616.brief: Societe Generale increases ICG holding to 6.34% voting rights, crossing major shareholder threshold on 15 July 2026.
17692616.take: Routine disclosure of major shareholding change. Market likely views this as a neutral event—institutional accumulation with no control implications unless stake rises further toward 10% threshold.

17692618.summary: Young & Co's Brewery executed its announced share buyback programme during 13-16 July 2026 through Peel Hunt on the London Stock Exchange. The company purchased 7,649 Non-voting shares at a volume-weighted average price of 718.24p (range 716-748p) for cancellation, and 10,250 A shares at 920.76p average (range 910-931p) for treasury. Post-transaction, the company will have 22,569,749 Non-voting shares and 38,037,755 A shares in issue.
17692618.brief: Young & Co's Brewery completes share buyback, purchasing 7,649 Non-voting and 10,250 A shares between 13-16 July.
17692618.take: Routine capital management activity consistent with announced buyback programme; modest transaction size suggests measured approach to share reduction, likely neutral market reception.

17692620.summary: Foresight Group Holdings Limited bought back 214,238 ordinary shares between 10-16 July 2026 via Berenberg, with prices ranging from 445.50p to 475.00p. The volume-weighted average prices ranged from 451.98p to 471.64p across five trading days. Total treasury holdings now stand at 5,020,091 shares (non-voting), with 111,327,712 shares carrying voting rights out of 116,347,803 in issue. Cumulative buybacks under the current programme total 6,671,775 shares.
17692620.brief: Foresight Group purchased 214,238 shares at 452-475p under its April 2025 buyback programme.
17692620.take: Routine capital management disclosure showing consistent buyback execution. Share prices trending upward (472p+ on final day) may signal confidence, but buyback programmes are typically pre-authorised and non-event unless scale or pace changes materially.

17692816.summary: Valterra Platinum expects headline earnings of R18.5bn–R22.2bn (vs R1.2bn prior year) and headline EPS of 7,047–8,456 cents (vs 473 cents), representing >1,388% growth. Basic earnings projected at R18.6bn–R22.3bn (vs R0.6bn) with EPS of 7,085–8,494 cents (vs 223 cents), up >3,076%. Growth driven by 18% higher PGM sales volumes from recovered Amandelbult production post-flooding and rescheduled maintenance, plus 85% increase in dollar PGM basket price to $2,801/oz, translating to 66% rand price uplift. Results due 29 July 2026.
17692816.brief: Valterra Platinum reports H1 2026 earnings up >1,300%, driven by 18% volume growth and 85% stronger PGM prices.
17692816.take: Substantial earnings beat driven primarily by commodity price strength and volume recovery—supportive for equity but highlights cyclical exposure; market likely views positively given scale of beat.

17692582.summary: Vesari Inc., in which Tekcapital holds 51%, has appointed Dr. Joseph N. Moore to its newly formed Science Advisory Board. Moore is a renowned geothermal expert with five decades' experience, former Managing Principal Investigator of Utah's $300 million DOE-funded FORGE programme, and recipient of multiple prestigious geothermal industry awards. Vesari is developing technology for geothermal-powered hyperscale AI compute infrastructure, and Moore will guide subsurface and reservoir strategy as the company assembles advisors across geothermal energy, power management, and satellite technology.
17692582.brief: Tekcapital portfolio company Vesari appoints leading geothermal scientist Dr. Joseph N. Moore to Science Advisory Board.
17692582.take: Credibility boost for early-stage Vesari through high-profile scientific appointment, but announcement remains operational update with no financial metrics or commercial milestones; unlikely to meaningfully move Tekcapital share price without broader commercialisation progress.

17692592.summary: MicroSalt PLC (AIM: SALT) announced a live investor presentation on Tuesday 11 August 2026 at 2pm BST featuring CEO Rick Guiney and Interim CFO Gary Urmston. The presentation, open to existing and potential shareholders via Investor Meet Company, allows pre-submission of questions until 10 August. MicroSalt manufactures low-sodium salt with 50% reduced sodium content using patented micron-sized particles, targeting the £10+ billion global salt market with a granted US patent and 14 pending applications worldwide.
17692592.brief: MicroSalt to host investor presentation on 11 August via Investor Meet Company platform with CEO and CFO.
17692592.take: Routine corporate engagement event; non-material announcement flagged as having no significant impact. Investors should monitor actual presentation content for substantive updates on commercialisation progress or market traction.

17692591.summary: Conduit Holdings Limited (LSE: CRE), the parent of Bermuda-based multi-line reinsurer Conduit Re, will release interim financial results for the six months ended 30 June 2026 on 29 July 2026 at 7:00 am UK time. Management will host separate presentations: an analyst and investor webcast/call at 12:00 pm UK time and a retail investor presentation via Investor Meet Company at 4:00 pm UK time. The company holds an A- Financial Strength Rating from A.M. Best with stable outlook.
17692591.brief: Conduit Holdings to release H1 2026 interim results on 29 July with analyst presentations and retail investor session scheduled.
17692591.take: Routine disclosure of results timing and presentation arrangements; no new financial information or material disclosed in this notice. Market impact depends on actual H1 results when released.

17692590.summary: Swedbank posted Q2 2026 net profit of SEK 7,195m (down 2% QoQ) with return on equity of 14.2%. H1 2026 profit fell 10% to SEK 14,540m versus H1 2025 (SEK 16,082m). Total income grew 6% QoQ to SEK 18,104m, driven by higher lending volumes and asset management commissions. Costs rose 14% QoQ to SEK 7,854m including SEK 860m extraordinary restructuring charges. Credit impairments doubled to SEK 313m. Post-quarter, Swedbank settled all DFS investigations with a USD 50m payment for failure to disclose in 2016 and 2018. Common Equity Tier 1 ratio stood at 17.4%.
17692590.brief: Swedbank reports Q2 2026 profit of SEK 7.2bn; settles DFS investigation for USD 50m.
17692590.take: Strong operational performance with volume growth offset by higher costs and normalizing credit impairments; DFS settlement removes regulatory overhang. Likely positive read given closure of historical compliance issues, though profit decline reflects restructuring headwinds.

17692589.summary: Saga plc's Board has approved PwC as the Company's independent external auditor for the financial year ending 31 January 2028, following a formal tender process by the Audit and Risk Committee. The appointment will be recommended to shareholders at the 2027 AGM. KPMG will continue as auditor for the year ending 31 January 2027. The Board thanked KPMG for its service since 2017 and acknowledged all firms participating in the tender process.
17692589.brief: Saga to appoint PwC as external auditor from FY2028, replacing KPMG after nine years of service.
17692589.take: Routine auditor rotation following competitive tender; demonstrates governance rigour but signals no concerns about incumbent KPMG, likely a non-event for investors.

17692588.summary: Revolution Beauty Group PLC will release audited full year results for the year ended 28 February 2026 on Tuesday, 21 July 2026 at 11:00 am BST. CEO Tom Allsworth and CFO Neil Catto will host a live presentation on the Investor Meet Company platform. The global mass beauty business operates across c.17,500 retail doors in the UK, USA and international markets, employs 231 people, and combines DTC e-commerce with wholesale distribution channels.
17692588.brief: Revolution Beauty to announce FY2026 results on 21 July with live investor presentation via Investor Meet Company platform.
17692588.take: Routine results announcement notice; scheduling of earnings call is standard practice and provides no directional insight into FY2026 performance. Market impact will depend entirely on actual results disclosure.

17692587.summary: Safestay PLC has issued notice of its 2026 Annual General Meeting to be held on 12 August 2026 at 09:00 GMT at Safestay London Elephant & Castle, 144-152 Walworth Road, London SE17 1JL. The company's board unanimously recommends all proposed resolutions. Shareholders are encouraged to vote by proxy, with voting open until 09:00 on 10 August 2026. The 2025 Annual Report and Financial Statements are available on the investor relations portal at safestay.com/investors/reports.
17692587.brief: Safestay plc announces Annual General Meeting scheduled for 12 August 2026 at London Elephant & Castle location.
17692587.take: Routine disclosure; no read-through. Standard AGM notice with procedural voting information; contains no material business updates or strategic announcements affecting investor thesis.

17692585.summary: MFX announced the share price for its 2025 dividend comprising a 0.5197p scrip dividend (with cash or share election option) and 0.2599p bonus issue, both approved at the June AGM. The dividend share price of 22.1983p was calculated using the lowest daily VWAP over 20 dealing days pre-ex-dividend date, capped at 20% discount to ex-date VWAP. Payment occurs 20 August 2026 to shareholders on register 10 July 2026, with scrip election deadline 27 July 2026.
17692585.brief: Manx Financial Group sets scrip dividend and bonus issue share price at 22.1983p per share for 2025 distributions.
17692585.take: Routine administrative disclosure setting terms for approved dividend payments; no material news or strategic implications for investors.

17692584.summary: Anpario plc submitted its block listing return for four employee share schemes (SAYE, EMI, Unapproved, and Company Share Option Plan) for the period 17 January to 16 July 2026. No securities were issued or allotted during the period, and no increases to scheme allocations were applied for. Unallocated balances remain: SAYE 4,579 shares, EMI 57,373 shares, Unapproved 60,000 shares, and Company Share Option Plan 19,200 shares.
17692584.brief: Anpario PLC files block listing return for employee share schemes covering January-July 2026 period.
17692584.take: Routine regulatory disclosure with no material share issuance activity. The absence of allocations suggests limited current employee participation or utilisation of schemes.

17692583.summary: Alumasc Group PLC has suspended CEO Pamela Bingham pending a full investigation into matters relating to her professional conduct, supported by professional advisors. Non-Executive Chair Vijay Thakrar will temporarily assume Executive Chair responsibilities. The management team includes divisional managing directors and CFO with approximately 35 years combined tenure at Alumasc. The Group reported resilient performance despite challenging market conditions and enters the new financial year with a significantly expanded order book.
17692583.brief: Alumasc CEO Pamela Bingham suspended pending investigation into professional conduct; Non-Executive Chair Vijay Thakrar assumes executive role.
17692583.take: Market likely to view negatively due to executive uncertainty and governance concerns, though operational continuity supported by experienced management team and strong order book provides some reassurance.

17692557.summary: Bridgepoint reported record H1 2026 results with underlying EBITDA up 77.6% to £227.3m, fee-paying AUM rising 32.7% to $58.4bn, and management fees up 22.8% to £254.4m. Performance-related earnings surged 109.5% to £120.7m, driven by successful fund exits including €16.6bn returned to investors (record). The group raised €26bn of its increased €28bn 2026 fundraising target. Announced acquisition of Kayne Anderson Real Estate (KARE) in late June to strengthen US real estate capabilities. Interim dividend 4.8p per share payable October 2026. Full-year guidance maintained; €28bn fundraising target, 13-16% management fee growth, 55-60% EBITDA margin for 2026/27.
17692557.brief: Bridgepoint Group H1 2026: Strong growth across fundraising, fee income, and returns; raised €26bn toward €28bn target.
17692557.take: Strong operational momentum and capital deployment activity supports guidance, but reported profit before tax down 29% due to acquisition-related exceptional costs and amortisation, masking underlying strength. Market likely reads as positive on strategy execution and growth trajectory despite near-term cost headwinds.

17692566.summary: Ash Mehta, Chief Financial Officer of Celebrus Technologies PLC (AIM: CLBS), purchased 40,000 ordinary shares on 16 July 2026 in two equal tranches at 102.17p and 102.10p per share, averaging 102.14p. Following the transaction, Mehta's total beneficial holding increased to 220,070 shares, representing approximately 0.58% of the company's voting rights. The purchase was executed on the London Stock Exchange AIM market.
17692566.brief: Celebrus CFO Ash Mehta purchases 40,000 shares at average price of 102.14p, increasing stake to 0.58%.
17692566.take: Insider buying by CFO signals management confidence in the data solutions business, though modest stake size limits significance as strong conviction signal for retail investors.

17692565.summary: Helix granted 8.55m options to directors and consultants on 17 July 2026. CEO Bo Sears received 2.75m options, Chairman Keith Spickelmier 2.75m, CFO Ryan Neates 1.1m, and NED Gregg Peters 750k, all at 22p strike expiring July 2031. Two business development consultants received 1.2m combined options with share-price hurdles (35p, 45p, 55p) over 20 consecutive days. Grants align with IPO management incentive strategy and recognise 18-month milestones including Rudyard Project production commencement in February 2026 and July 2026 Keyes acquisition completion.
17692565.brief: Helix Exploration grants 8.55m share options to directors and consultants at 22p exercise price.
17692565.take: Standard PDMR incentive grant tying management to equity upside; shows confidence post-production ramp and acquisition completion. Market likely views this neutrally as routine capital management during operational ramp-up phase.

17692564.summary: Riverstone Credit Opportunities Income plc's unaudited NAV per ordinary share remained flat at $0.90 as of 30 June 2026 versus 31 March 2026. The energy infrastructure credit fund maintains a portfolio of 100% floating-rate, senior secured loans with 36.5% cumulative NAV total return since May 2019 IPO and 43.7 cents in cumulative dividends paid. Current portfolio includes 3 unrealised investments valued at $30.1mm and 23 realised investments generating $257.9mm gross realised capital with average 1.20x gross MOIC.
17692564.brief: RCOI reports Q2 2026 NAV held steady at $0.90 per share; cumulative 36.5% NAV total return since IPO.
17692564.take: Flat quarterly NAV suggests portfolio stability but underwhelming near-term momentum; continued dividend generation supports income investors, but zero NAV growth may disappoint growth-oriented holders.

17692558.summary: Malvern International PLC (AIM: MLVN) has cancelled 2.15m existing share options previously granted to management at 10p-24p exercise prices, deeming them ineffective retention tools. The company granted 2.635m new options to 12 staff members (including CEO Richard Mace with 520,000 options, CFO-designate Daniel Chaffer with 380,000, and Chief Development Officer Stephen Harvey with 100,000) at 21p exercise price, representing 7.9% of current issued capital. Options vest in tranches over two-three years at share price milestones of 45p, 80p, 120p and 150p, with ten-year lifespans. The exercise price reflects the closing share price on 15 July 2026.
17692558.brief: Malvern International cancels old share options and grants 2.6m new options to 12 managers at 21p exercise price with tiered vesting targets.
17692558.take: Restructured incentives signal management confidence in growth trajectory and better align executive interests with shareholders. Market likely views neutrally as routine governance; no material dilution concern given reduced option pool.

17692562.summary: ITM Power's Chief Executive Officer Dennis Schulz and Chief Technology Officer Simon Bourne each purchased 134 partnership shares and received 134 matching shares on 15 July 2026 under the company's Buy as You Earn Share Incentive Plan, acquiring 268 shares each at £1.1231 per share. The scheme allows employees to buy up to £150 of shares monthly with one-for-one company matching.
17692562.brief: ITM Power execs acquire shares via staff scheme at £1.12 per share in July
17692562.take: Routine share scheme participation by executives; indicates confidence in company but non-material in isolation; typical of BAYE administrative disclosures with no predictive value.

17692561.summary: Alumasc delivered FY26 revenue of £107m (FY25: £113m) and underlying PBT of £10m (FY25: £14m), broadly meeting revised guidance despite strengthened demand headwinds in final quarter. Order book grew 49% year-on-year driven by commercial strategy refocus. Housebuilding Products revenue increased 16%; Building Envelope stable; Water Management declined 16% (3% excluding Hong Kong CLK project). Net bank debt leverage remains strong at 0.5x with £7m net debt.
17692561.brief: Alumasc reports resilient FY26 performance with £107m revenue and £10m UPBT despite macro headwinds; order book up 49%.
17692561.take: Mixed read: strong order book growth and operational improvements offset by lower profitability and revenue decline, with macro uncertainty persisting. Market likely neutral-to-cautious given guidance met but earnings down and Water Management headwinds ongoing.

17692559.summary: Kodal (49% stake in KMUK, which holds 65% of mining operator LMLB) reported Q2 production of 26,174 DMT spodumene concentrate grading 5.34% Li₂O, with year-to-date output of 53,195 DMT. Third shipment (20,400 tonnes) arrived June 27 at US$2,304/DMT equivalent; fourth shipment (24,200 tonnes) departed July 11. LMLB repaid US$13m loan during quarter, with KMUK using proceeds to reduce Hainan debt. Production underperformed due to May crushing circuit maintenance; operations normalized in June. Phase Two floatation plant development continues.
17692559.brief: Kodal Minerals' Bougouni lithium project produces 26,174 tonnes Q2 spodumene concentrate; receives first US$13m loan repayment from operator.
17692559.take: Positive momentum: operational production ramping post-maintenance, regular exports materializing at strong lithium prices, debt reduction progressing, and mine operator generating sufficient cashflow for loan repayment demonstrates project viability.

17692563.summary: BlackRock, Inc. crossed a notification threshold in Supermarket Income REIT PLC on 15 July 2026, with total voting rights increasing to 7.23% (90.3m votes) from 6.69% previously. Holdings comprise 4.97% direct shares (62m), 1.56% via securities lending (19.5m), and 0.70% via cash-settled CFDs (8.7m). The increase reflects acquisition activity through multiple controlled undertakings across jurisdictions.
17692563.brief: BlackRock reduces Supermarket Income REIT stake from 6.69% to 7.23% via shareholding and financial instruments.
17692563.take: Routine major holding disclosure indicating passive institutional investor activity; no strategic signal. Slight increase in already-significant stake unlikely to materially impact share price absent broader context.

17692573.summary: EnSilica's Remuneration Committee approved options over ordinary shares to executives: Mark Hodgkins (Chairman, 393,000 options), Ian Lankshear (CEO, 1,212,000 options), and Kristoff Rademan (CFO, 561,000 options). Total of 2.166m options granted at 0.1p exercise price. Vesting occurs following FY2029 results if adjusted EBITDA reaches £10-13m and average share price hits 150-200p, with extension to FY2030 at higher thresholds (£12-15m EBITDA, 172-252p share price). 50% of vested shares subject to one-year holding period.
17692573.brief: EnSilica grants share options to three executives under LTIP 2022, with vesting tied to EBITDA and TSR targets through 2029-2030.
17692573.take: Standard long-term incentive scheme aligning management with shareholder value over three years; demonstrates confidence in growth trajectory but depends on execution of ambitious financial targets.

17692572.summary: Dr Miles Jakeman AM steps down as chairman and director after nine years leading GetBusy through its 2017 IPO and transformation into a focused SaaS and AI business. Paul Huberman, a chartered accountant who has been non-executive director and audit committee chairman since 2020, succeeds him immediately. Huberman previously served as finance director at three London-listed companies and currently holds non-executive roles. Nigel Payne returns as audit committee chairman, a position he held 2017-2020.
17692572.brief: GetBusy appoints Paul Huberman as chairman, replacing Dr Miles Jakeman after nine years.
17692572.take: Planned succession with internal promotion of experienced finance executive signals continuity and governance stability; market likely neutral as transition is orderly and successor is well-qualified.

17692571.summary: Polar Capital granted conditional share awards under its 2026 Executive Incentive Plan to CEO Iain Evans (117,005 shares), CFO Samir Ayub (69,077 shares), CIO Alexander Black (69,077 shares), and COO Nicholas Farren (58,343 shares). Awards valued at 899p average share price vest after three years, subject to continued employment and performance metrics: 35% tied to adjusted diluted EPS targets, 35% to FTSE 250 relative TSR, 10% to net inflows from key geographies, and 20% to strategic progress. Vested shares subject to two-year holding period post-vesting.
17692571.brief: Polar Capital grants LTIP awards to four executives on 16 July 2026, with vesting after three years subject to performance conditions.
17692571.take: Routine disclosure of executive compensation with standard performance hurdles and extended holding periods. Market typically views this neutrally as expected management incentive alignment, though concentrated awards suggest board confidence in leadership.

17692569.summary: Adepeju Adebajo, Independent Non-Executive Director at Ibstock PLC since 2021, has notified the Board of her intention to resign effective 23 September 2026 to pursue other opportunities. Chair Richard Akers thanked her for valuable contributions. The Nomination Committee will lead the search for a successor, with further announcements to follow.
17692569.brief: Ibstock's independent non-executive director Adepeju Adebajo to step down 23 September 2026.
17692569.take: Routine board transition with adequate notice period; successor search already commencing suggests minimal disruption to governance. Likely non-event for market.

17692568.summary: Executive Richard Tremblay exercised 100,000 share options (at C$3.06) and immediately sold 100,000 common shares at an average price of C$11.14 per share (£5.86) on July 15, 2026. The transaction was conducted on the Toronto Stock Exchange and NYSE American. Trekor is a North American copper producer with operations including the Gibraltar Mine in British Columbia and Florence Copper in Arizona, which recently commenced commercial production.
17692568.brief: Trekor Metals executive Richard Tremblay exercised 100,000 options and sold 100,000 shares at C$11.14/share on July 15.
17692568.take: Insider selling at current market levels; while not necessarily bearish, simultaneous option exercise and full share sale-off suggests limited conviction in near-term upside by management.

17692567.summary: European Green Transition PLC awarded options over 11,833,333 shares to Executive Chairman Cathal Friel (6,666,667 options) and CFO Jack Kelly (4,166,667 options), plus 571,000 options to Wind Energy Services Managing Director David Broadbank. All awards carry nominal 0.25p exercise price and vest after three years subject to continued employment and revenue growth targets. Options represent 4.4% of current issued share capital. Awards include standard malus, clawback, and leaver provisions designed to retain key executives and align with shareholder interests.
17692567.brief: EGT grants 12.4m share options to executives and management, representing 4.4% of issued capital, with three-year vesting.
17692567.take: Routine retention incentive aligned with post-acquisition integration strategy; demonstrates confidence in Wind Energy Services growth targets but dilution of 4.4% may concern some shareholders. Largely non-event given standard terms and performance conditions.

17692586.summary: Odyssean Investment Trust PLC has applied to the London Stock Exchange for a block listing of 5,000,000 ordinary shares of 1 pence each, effective 20 July 2026. This augments the existing block listing with 1,560,921 remaining shares. The new shares will be reserved and issued to meet market demand and manage the premium at which shares trade, consistent with the company's premium management policy.
17692586.brief: Odyssean Investment Trust applies for 5m share block listing on LSE to manage premium to NAV.
17692586.take: Standard capital management tool; allows controlled issuance to prevent excessive premiums. Routine process for investment trusts, typically viewed as shareholder-friendly premium management.

17692529.summary: Glanbia plc purchased 13,224 ordinary shares on Euronext Dublin at a volume-weighted average price of €23.0151 per share, with prices ranging from €22.78 to €23.20. The shares will be cancelled. This transaction is part of the company's €50 million share buyback programme announced February 25, 2026, and scheduled to complete by September 30, 2026. Following settlement, Glanbia will have 238,922,743 ordinary shares in issue.
17692529.brief: Glanbia repurchased 13,224 shares at €23.02 average price on July 16, progressing its €50m buyback programme.
17692529.take: Share buybacks typically signal management confidence in valuation and provide modest capital discipline; routine execution suggests steady programme progression with no material market-moving implications.

17692530.summary: Greencoat Renewables PLC purchased 760 ordinary shares on Euronext Dublin on 16 July 2026 at prices ranging from €0.7510 to €0.7570 per share, with a volume-weighted average price of €0.7570. The shares will be cancelled. Following settlement, the company holds 200,000 treasury shares and has 1,083,472,954 shares in issue. The purchase forms part of the company's share buyback programme announced 5 March 2026.
17692530.brief: Greencoat Renewables repurchased 760 shares at €0.7510-€0.7570 on 16 July as part of its March 2026 buyback programme.
17692530.take: Routine share buyback execution; modest scale (760 shares) and consistent pricing suggest orderly capital management rather than material market signal.

17692531.summary: M.P. Evans Group PLC purchased 2,390 of its own shares through Cavendish Capital Markets for cancellation on 16 July 2026. Shares were bought at prices ranging from 1,612p to 1,658p per share, with a volume-weighted average price of 1,632.53p. Following cancellation, the company now has 52,162,259 shares in issue, representing total voting rights for Disclosure and Transparency Rules purposes.
17692531.brief: M.P. Evans repurchased 2,390 shares for cancellation on 16 July 2026 at an average price of 1,632.53p.
17692531.take: Routine share buyback execution; modest volume reflects ongoing capital management. No material market implications from this incremental repurchase.

17692532.summary: AEP Plantations purchased 57,471 ordinary shares on 16 July 2026 at prices ranging from 166p to 173p, with a volume-weighted average of 168.5p, through Cavendish Capital Markets. Since the buyback programme's announcement on 6 July, the company has acquired 420,031 shares in aggregate, now held in treasury. Following this transaction, 399,762,720 shares remain in issue with 383,173,449 voting rights outstanding.
17692532.brief: AEP Plantations repurchased 57,471 shares on 16 July at 166-173p as part of its announced buyback programme.
17692532.take: Routine capital management activity; share buybacks typically signal management confidence but have minimal immediate market impact absent broader context on capital allocation strategy.

17692533.summary: Science Group plc purchased 15,000 of its 1 pence ordinary shares at 585 pence per share on 16 July 2026, representing 0.04% of voting rights. The shares were bought through Panmure Liberum Limited via AIMX at 11:33:31 and will be held in treasury. Following the purchase, 40,687,593 ordinary shares remain in issue (excluding 5,498,281 treasury shares).
17692533.brief: Science Group PLC repurchased 15,000 shares at 585p per share on 16 July 2026 for treasury.
17692533.take: Routine share buyback disclosed under Market Abuse Regulation; modest scale suggests continuation of existing capital management programme with no material implications.

17692528.summary: Pantheon International PLC purchased 443,519 ordinary shares on 16 July 2026 via J.P. Morgan Securities at prices ranging from 386.50p to 389.00p, with a weighted average of 387.66p. The company intends to cancel these shares. Following the transaction, 394,780,819 ordinary shares remain in issue with total voting rights of 394,780,819.
17692528.brief: Pantheon International repurchased 443,519 shares at 386.50-389.00p on 16 July, intending cancellation.
17692528.take: Routine share buyback and cancellation programme execution; signals capital management and potential shareholder return but represents modest share count reduction of 0.1%.

17692527.summary: AdvancedAdvT Limited (AIM: ADVT) purchased 25,000 ordinary shares on 16 July 2026 at 160.00 pence per share, to be held in treasury. The cumulative buyback programme total now reaches 5,253,100 shares since March 2026. Following the purchase, total voting rights stand at 131,872,706 shares out of 137,125,806 issued shares.
17692527.brief: AdvancedAdvT completes further share buyback of 25,000 shares at 160p, bringing cumulative total to 5.25m shares.
17692527.take: Routine buyback execution within previously announced programme; modest share count reduction supports potential EPS accretion but signals no material strategic development.

17692525.summary: Babcock International Group purchased 14,665 ordinary shares on 16 July 2026 through J.P. Morgan Securities plc at prices ranging from 1,006.5p to 1,028.5p, with a volume-weighted average price of 1,020.52p. Since 1 July 2026, the company has acquired 855,054 shares for £8.6m. The company holds 13.4m treasury shares with 492.2m shares in issue. Shares will be held in treasury.
17692525.brief: Babcock International repurchased 14,665 shares on 16 July 2026 at average price of 1,020.52p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
17692525.take: Routine share buyback execution within disclosed programme; demonstrates capital discipline but represents incremental shareholder return in context of operational performance—non-event absent broader strategic signals.

17692524.summary: HICL Infrastructure PLC purchased 100,000 ordinary shares through Investec Bank plc on 16 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 134.3548p (range 134.00p-135.40p). The company now holds 166.5 million treasury shares, with 1.86 billion shares in issue excluding treasury holdings. HICL intends to retain these shares as treasury stock.
17692524.brief: HICL Infrastructure repurchased 100,000 shares at average price of 134.35p on 16 July.
17692524.take: Routine share buyback within an established programme; modest scale suggests capital management rather than strategic signal; likely neutral market reception as no new information on operational performance or strategy.

17692523.summary: HarbourVest Global Private Equity Limited purchased 7,000 ordinary shares on 16 July 2026 at an average price of £33.544 per share for cancellation, pursuant to authority granted at its 15 July 2026 AGM. Following this transaction, the company has 69,626,352 shares in issue. The updated share count serves as the denominator for FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules notifications.
17692523.brief: HVPE repurchased 7,000 shares at £33.54 per share for cancellation; share count now 69.6m.
17692523.take: Routine share buyback for cancellation; modest transaction size (£235k) suggests capital management rather than strategic signal; market likely treats as non-event given scale relative to £2.3bn+ market cap.

17692521.summary: International Workplace Group PLC acquired 154,000 ordinary shares on 16 July 2026 at volume-weighted average price of 1.9205p across multiple venues. Total programme purchases since December 2025 now stand at 39.86m shares. Following this tranche, 954.43m shares remain in issue (excluding treasury). Shares will be cancelled or held in treasury per shareholder authority granted at May 2026 AGM.
17692521.brief: IWG purchased 154,000 shares on 16 July as part of ongoing £297m buyback programme initiated December 2025.
17692521.take: Routine share buyback execution within pre-approved programme; demonstrates capital returns discipline but immaterial to fundamentals without context on valuation rationale or broader capital allocation strategy.

17692519.summary: Edinburgh Investment Trust PLC purchased 50,000 ordinary shares on 16 July 2026 via Investec Bank at an average price of 827.80p per share, intending to hold them in treasury. The company now holds 69,699,709 shares in treasury out of 195,666,734 issued shares, leaving 125,967,025 shares with voting rights outstanding.
17692519.brief: Edinburgh Investment Trust repurchased 50,000 shares at 827.80p on 16 July, holding them in treasury.
17692519.take: Routine share buyback within normal capital management; modest scale repurchase suggests modest shareholder value accretion, market unlikely to react significantly.

17692520.summary: On 16 July 2026, Barings Emerging EMEA Opportunities PLC purchased 1,493 of its own ordinary shares on the London Stock Exchange at a weighted average price of 808.45 pence per share from J.P. Morgan Securities plc. The shares will be cancelled. Following the transaction, the company holds 3,318,207 treasury shares and has 11,491,412 ordinary shares in issue.
17692520.brief: Barings Emerging EMEA Opportunities repurchased 1,493 shares at 808.45p per share for cancellation.
17692520.take: Routine share buyback for cancellation; modest scale suggests capital management rather than material signalling; typical for investment companies but immaterial to valuation.

17692534.summary: J Sainsbury PLC purchased 3,775,000 ordinary shares between 10–16 July 2026 via Shore Capital Stockbrokers for £13,080,544 (average price 346.5p). Transactions spanned multiple venues (XLON, CHIX, BATE, AQXE, TRQX) with prices ranging 337.8p–354.3p. Purchases form part of the buyback programme authorised 23 April 2026. All shares will be cancelled. Full execution details disclosed per MAR requirements.
17692534.brief: Sainsbury bought 3.775m shares (£13.1m) over six days ending 16 July as part of existing buyback programme.
17692534.take: Routine share buyback execution disclosure within pre-announced programme. Continued capital returns signal confidence but no material news; standard market practice.

17692536.summary: Aircraft leasing company Avation PLC repurchased 20,000 ordinary shares on 16 July 2026 at 137.5 pence per share, holding them in treasury. Following the transaction, 61,425,369 shares remain in issue with 1,600,068 held in treasury, reducing voting rights to 59,825,301. The repurchase is conducted under an approved buyback mandate authorising up to 25% of issued capital, approved at the November 2025 AGM and valid until the next AGM.
17692536.brief: Avation repurchases 20,000 shares at 137.5p under approved buyback programme.
17692536.take: Routine share buyback execution within approved programme; modest scale suggests measured capital management; no material positive or negative signal for share price.

17692537.summary: Metir plc has entered a non-binding Memorandum of Understanding with Portsmouth Aviation Limited's Paqua trading division to evaluate integrated water treatment and monitoring solutions. The collaboration combines Paqua's PaquaVida water purification platform—capable of producing up to 300,000 litres of potable water daily—with Metir's Modern Water Microtox® biological monitoring technology. Initial focus targets Gulf Cooperation Council markets where Metir already operates 27 Continuous Toxicity Monitors in Doha's drinking water network, with expansion to UK and international markets under evaluation.
17692537.brief: Metir PLC signs MOU with Portsmouth Aviation's Paqua division to combine water purification and biological monitoring technologies.
17692537.take: Early-stage partnership exploration with complementary technologies in high-growth water infrastructure markets; positive signal for addressable opportunity but non-binding MOU carries execution risk and provides no immediate commercial commitment or revenue visibility.

17692538.summary: Smiths News has won new long-term contracts with Frontline Limited and Seymour Distribution Limited, extending its appointment as exclusive national wholesale distributor through April 2037. The agreements expand distribution territories across Great Britain from April 2030, with combined Frontline and Seymour portfolios representing over 60% of the UK magazine market. Expected incremental revenue uplift of approximately £105m per annum from expanded territories. This is the fourth major contract award in recent months, alongside News UK and Associated Newspapers contracts announced in June 2026, securing substantial long-term volume visibility across newspapers and magazines.
17692538.brief: Smiths News secures major long-term contracts with Frontline and Seymour, adding £105m annual revenue from April 2030.
17692538.take: Significant positive: secures major revenue visibility (£105m annually from 2030), market-leading position across 60%+ of magazine sector, and extended contract certainty through 2037 strengthens competitive moat and earnings predictability.

17692539.summary: The Renewables Infrastructure Group Limited purchased 270,000 ordinary shares on 16 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 74.0018p through Investec Bank plc, with prices ranging from 73.55p to 74.20p. The shares will be held as treasury stock. TRIG now holds 153.2 million treasury shares, with 2.33 billion voting shares outstanding excluding treasury stock.
17692539.brief: TRIG buys back 270,000 shares at 74p as part of its ongoing €2024 programme.
17692539.take: Routine share buyback execution under existing authorised programme; neutral signal suggesting capital management but no material strategic implications for investors.

17692540.summary: On 16 July 2026, Grafton Group plc repurchased 60,000 ordinary shares for cancellation via Goodbody Stockbrokers at a volume-weighted average price of £9.3154, with a range of £9.1490 to £9.4140. This brings total shares purchased under the £25m buyback programme announced 30 June 2026 to 779,174 shares. The buyback commenced 30 June 2026 and is being executed on the London Stock Exchange.
17692540.brief: Grafton Group purchases 60,000 shares at £9.32 average as part of £25m buyback programme.
17692540.take: Share buybacks typically signal management confidence in valuation and reduce share count, supporting EPS accretion. Routine capital allocation activity with modest positive undertone but no material catalyst.

17692541.summary: Alumni Capital Limited has exercised its right to convert £100,000 principal plus £10,000 finance charge from convertible loan notes into 11,763,447 ordinary shares at 0.9351 pence per share (15% discount to 10-day VWAP). The investor simultaneously receives 6,786,604 CLN Warrants exercisable at 1.21563 pence. Post-conversion, Sunda Energy will have 456.5 million ordinary shares in issue. Admission to AIM expected 20 July 2026.
17692541.brief: Sunda Energy converts £100k loan notes into 11.8m shares at 0.9351p, grants 6.8m warrants at 1.21563p.
17692541.take: Debt-to-equity conversion signals refinancing but dilutes existing shareholders materially; warrant grant creates future dilution overhang. Routine capital restructuring typical of cash-constrained exploration companies, neither inherently bullish nor bearish.

17692542.summary: Wise Group reported Q1 FY27 results with cross-border volume growing 26% YoY to $69.3 billion and active customers increasing 21% to 11.863 million. Net revenue rose 25% YoY to $714.0 million, with transaction revenue up 27% to $540.9 million. Customer holdings grew 31% to $41.2 billion. The cross-border take rate compressed 2 basis points to 50 basis points as Wise invested operating leverage in competitive pricing. The company reiterated FY27 guidance: net revenue growth around the middle of its 15-20% medium-term target range and income before tax margin around the top of the 20-25% range.
17692542.brief: Wise Q1 FY27: 26% cross-border volume growth to $69.3bn, 21% customer growth to 11.9m, net revenue up 25% to $714m.
17692542.take: Strong growth across all metrics with strategic margin compression to strengthen competitive positioning; guidance reiteration provides confidence but suggests management sees challenging conditions ahead.

17692543.summary: InterContinental Hotels Group purchased 1,000 ordinary shares on 16 July 2026 through Goldman Sachs International on the London Stock Exchange at prices ranging from $159.00 to $160.50, with a volume-weighted average of $159.6016. The shares will be cancelled, reducing shares in issue to 148,611,282 (excluding 5,431,782 treasury shares). The buyback was authorised at the May 2025 AGM and undertaken per instructions issued 17 February 2026.
17692543.brief: IHG repurchased 1,000 shares at average $159.60 on 16 July; intends to cancel them.
17692543.take: Routine share buyback execution; modest 1,000 share purchase suggests ongoing capital management programme. No material market significance given scale relative to 148m+ shares outstanding.

17692544.summary: System1's board continues to reject Brave Bison's revised offer comprising 2.7553 Brave Bison shares plus 68p cash per System1 share (317p total value, 4% premium to 10 July close of 305p). The board consulted major shareholders who confirmed the offer materially undervalues the company. System1 highlights the offer lacks a customary control premium and references Brave Bison's misleading premium calculations omitting System1's positive 16 March 2026 trading update. The company reported record H2 FY26 revenue, increased dividends, and positive FY27 outlook in 8 July results.
17692544.brief: System1 unanimously rejects Brave Bison's revised 317p takeover offer, citing undervaluation and insufficient control premium.
17692544.take: Ongoing takeover resistance with management backed by major shareholder feedback; likely negative sentiment toward Brave Bison's proposal; signals potential bidding war or deal collapse ahead.

17692545.summary: Burberry Group delivered Q1 FY27 comparable retail sales growth of 5% with retail revenue of £455m (up 5% reported, 4% at constant exchange rates). Americas grew 12%, Greater China 9%, and Asia Pacific 3%, though EMEIA declined 3% due to Middle East conflict impacts. First growth across all divisions in three years, anchored by outerwear outperformance. CEO Joshua Schulman highlighted brand momentum from the 'Portraits of an Icon' campaign, new customer acquisition (19% increase in rainwear customers, Gen-Z up double-digit), and successful product strategy. FY27 guidance expects revenue growth and margin expansion with £100m annualised cost savings target and £20m currency tailwind.
17692545.brief: Burberry reports Q1 FY27 comparable retail sales up 5%, driven by Americas and Greater China growth with momentum in outerwear and accessories.
17692545.take: Market likely reads positively: luxury brand demonstrating genuine momentum across regions and categories post-restructuring, with underlying sales growth and margin improvement trajectory intact despite geopolitical headwinds.

17692546.summary: Eagle Eye reported FY26 ARR growth of 31% to £44.5m, with group revenue of £46.7m (down 3% including NRS loss, but +21% excluding it). Adjusted EBITDA reached £9.8m at 21% margin, materially ahead of consensus expectations of £7.0m. The company secured major new customers including easyJet, Subway, and a top UK health & beauty retailer, plus first OEM partnership contracts. Net cash strengthened to £16.1m. Management targets double-digit revenue and EBITDA growth in FY27, with medium-term ambitions of £100m+ revenue and 30%+ EBITDA margin.
17692546.brief: Eagle Eye beats FY26 expectations with 31% ARR growth and £9.8m adjusted EBITDA, exceeding market forecasts.
17692546.take: Strong operational recovery post-NRS loss with beat on profitability and momentum in customer wins likely resonates positively; positive cash generation and OEM progress support growth narrative, though near-term guidance remains cautiously phased.

17692547.summary: Plus500 purchased 63,537 ordinary shares on 16 July 2026 via Panmure Liberum at prices ranging from 4,196p to 4,308p, with a volume-weighted average of 4,253.95p. Shares will be held in treasury. Following the purchase, 69,049,261 shares remain in issue (excluding treasury) and the company holds 45,839,116 treasury shares. Total voting rights stand at 69,049,261.
17692547.brief: Plus500 repurchased 63,537 shares at average price of 4,253.95p under its ongoing buyback programme.
17692547.take: Routine share buyback execution under announced programme; no material implications for investors beyond modest capital allocation signal and potential future EPS accretion.

17692548.summary: Nova Ljubljanska Banka published an amended offering memorandum for its voluntary takeover of Addiko Bank AG, reducing the minimum acceptance threshold from 75% to 50% plus one share (9,750,001 shares) and extending the acceptance period to 29 July 2026. The offer price remains EUR 37.00 per share in cash, representing a 39.6% premium to the competing Raiffeisen Bank International bid. Addiko shareholders who previously accepted RBI's offer may revoke acceptance until 23 July and tender into NLB's offer.
17692548.brief: NLB lowers Addiko takeover bid threshold to 50%+1 share, extends acceptance to 29 July in competing offer vs RBI.
17692548.take: Lower threshold reduces execution risk for NLB and makes its offer more attractive to Addiko shareholders facing a bidding contest; suggests NLB confidence and potential closing likelihood, though regulatory and MAC conditions remain.

17692549.summary: Fulcrum Metals has achieved 100% ownership of Teck-Hughes gold tailings project and secured exclusive licence for cyanide-free processing technology from Extrakt. A 159-hole auger programme confirmed gold, silver, and critical minerals (tellurium, gallium, rubidium, strontium, zirconium) throughout tailings. The company secured £6m funding from Yorkville Advisors and a non-binding US$20m royalty financing term sheet from Chancery Royalty, contingent on successful pilot plant results. Key near-term priorities include completing pilot plant, delivering maiden NI 43-101 Mineral Resource Estimate, and advancing engineering and permitting.
17692549.brief: Fulcrum Metals reports strong progress on tailings processing technology, pilot plant execution, and £6m funding secured.
17692549.take: Market likely reads positively: company demonstrates clear technical progress, third-party validation via royalty provider interest, and substantial capital secured to advance pilot phase without equity dilution.

17692550.summary: Arkle's initial 1,500m RC drilling at Erongo Uranium Project, Namibia is proceeding as planned with 52 holes completed for 1,017m. Carnotite, the principal ore mineral for surficial uranium deposits, has been visually logged in three holes across two fence lines at depths of 4-15m. The company expanded the programme to include step-out holes on eastern paleochannel targets, test the western paleochannel target, and bring forward drilling on the ULG target from Q3 2026. First assay results expected early September 2026.
17692550.brief: Arkle Resources expands maiden uranium drilling programme in Namibia; carnotite mineralisation visually logged in three holes.
17692550.take: Early-stage exploration update with visual mineralisation confirmation warrants monitoring; however, no grade data yet limits significance. Market likely reads this positively as operational progress validating geological model and exploration strategy.

17692551.summary: Itaconix PLC reported H1 2026 unaudited revenues of $8.3 million, up 72% versus H1 2025 ($4.8m) and 46% versus H2 2025 ($5.7m), with growth across all product segments. Gross profit margin expected to remain at 36%, in line with FY 2025. The company raised full-year 2026 revenue guidance to no less than $14.8 million, ahead of prior analyst expectations of $13.3 million, while maintaining expectations for small positive EBITDA for FY 2026.
17692551.brief: Itaconix posts record H1 2026 revenue of $8.3m (+72% YoY) with FY guidance raised to minimum $14.8m.
17692551.take: Strong operational momentum with accelerating growth and raised guidance suggests market confidence in execution; however, guidance cushion ($14.8m vs $13.3m consensus) is modest and dependent on reorder sustainability.

17692552.summary: Experian purchased 243,433 ordinary shares on the London Stock Exchange on 16 July 2026 via BofA Securities Europe SA as part of a share repurchase programme announced 30 June 2026. Shares traded between 2,560p and 2,697p, with weighted average price of 2,645.72p. All purchased shares will be cancelled. Weekly transaction updates will be published on the company's website.
17692552.brief: Experian repurchased 243,433 shares on 16 July at average price of 2,645.72p under ongoing buyback programme.
17692552.take: Routine capital allocation disclosure showing ongoing execution of announced buyback; signals management confidence but represents standard shareholder return activity with no material implications.

17692535.summary: Thalia Therapeutics seeks shareholder approval today for the acquisition of Sanmirna Therapeutics and an oversubscribed £2.75 million fundraise. The deal adds miRisten, a Phase 1 microRNA therapeutic for Acute Myeloid Leukaemia, plus the Nuvec® delivery platform and a dual-targeting cardiovascular siRNA programme. The company expects miRisten Phase 1 topline data in H1 2027, with funding secured for planned work streams across the expanded portfolio.
17692535.brief: Thalia Therapeutics holds AGM to approve Sanmirna acquisition and £2.75m fundraise, advancing RNA therapeutics pipeline.
17692535.take: Material value-creation catalyst with funded pipeline de-risking clinical progression; acquisition strengthens portfolio and near-term data readouts ahead provide catalysts, though biotech execution risk remains.

17692556.summary: Taylor Maritime reported a net loss of $46.1m for year ended 31 March 2026, including $23.7m impairment charges, but achieved $22.0m adjusted EBITDA. The Board announced an orderly wind-down strategy in March 2026, having sold 23 vessels during the year for $381.1m (2.8% discount to fair value) and eliminated all bank debt. Total shareholder distributions since IPO reach $362m ($1.10/share). Post-period, the company announced a third compulsory redemption of $45m, with expectations to distribute majority of NAV by calendar year-end.
17692556.brief: Dry bulk shipper Taylor Maritime to wind down and return capital after selling majority of fleet.
17692556.take: Market likely views this negatively as a strategic retreat during a recovery phase. Orderly wind-down eliminates upside from market cycles; capital returns via redemptions may be inefficient versus potential growth, though balance sheet strength and debt elimination are positive.

17692555.summary: Nathan Boom becomes CEO effective 17 July 2026, reporting to Executive Chair Craig Ransley. Boom brings 20+ years finance/commercial experience, including 8.5 years at TerraCom where he served as CCO and CFO, overseeing Blair Athol acquisition and Universal Coal takeover. He receives 10 million fully paid ordinary shares as inducement. Nicholas Beal steps down as CEO but remains Board Executive Director and Company Secretary, focusing on regulatory compliance and governance.
17692555.brief: Amigo Resources appoints Nathan Boom as CEO; Nicholas Beal transitions to Executive Director and Company Secretary role.
17692555.take: Experienced operational appointment with track record in resource sector M&A and transformation. Market likely sees this positively as strengthening commercial execution capability for African mining expansion, though share issuance dilutes existing shareholders.

17692560.summary: M&C Saatchi purchased 50,000 ordinary shares at 138.0 pence per share from Panmure Liberum on 16 July 2026, continuing its share buyback programme announced 9 March 2026. Following settlement and cancellation, the company will have 120,903,435 shares in issue with 485,970 held in treasury, reducing voting rights to 120,417,465. The transaction was executed as a single trade at 09:47 UK time on AIM.
17692560.brief: M&C Saatchi completes share buyback of 50,000 shares at 138p per share on 16 July 2026.
17692560.take: Routine share buyback execution under existing programme; demonstrates capital discipline but represents marginal shareholder value accretion given modest purchase volume. No material implications for investors.

17692514.summary: GlobalData PLC purchased 93,812 ordinary shares on 16 July 2026 through Investec Bank at prices ranging from 72.0p to 76.2p, with a weighted average price of 73.24p. The shares will be cancelled, reducing issued share count to 745,809,786. This transaction forms part of the company's Share Buyback Programme that commenced 23 June 2026. No treasury shares are held.
17692514.brief: GlobalData repurchases 93,812 shares at 72-76.2p as part of ongoing buyback programme; shares to be cancelled.
17692514.take: Routine share buyback execution under pre-announced programme; modest single-day volume suggests steady, measured capital return to shareholders; no material market read-through.

17692515.summary: Gamma Communications purchased 16,425 ordinary shares on 16 July 2026 via Investec at prices ranging 936-948p, with weighted average of 942.10p. This is part of the buyback programme announced 13 January 2026. Total shares repurchased to date: 2,717,251. Post-settlement, 91,047,100 shares remain in issue with 1,514,022 held in treasury; 89,533,078 voting shares outstanding. Shares will be cancelled. Company remains in offer period (commenced 7 April 2026).
17692515.brief: Gamma Communications buys back 16,425 shares at 942p average as part of ongoing £XXm programme announced January 2026.
17692515.take: Routine capital management execution during offer period. Share buyback reduces share count and can modestly support EPS, but represents normal housekeeping under existing programme with no strategic signal.

17692517.summary: JSC Halyk Bank's Board of Directors has announced an Extraordinary General Shareholders' Meeting scheduled for August 20, 2026, conducted via absent (postal) voting without an in-person gathering. The shareholder register cutoff is July 20, 2026. The agenda comprises two items: approval of the meeting agenda itself and adoption of a dividend resolution on common shares with specific per-share amounts to be determined. Materials are available on the bank's website in Russian, Kazakh, and English. GDR holders should contact Bank of New York Mellon's depositary for voting instructions. No quorum requirement applies under absent voting procedures.
17692517.brief: JSC Halyk Bank convenes extraordinary general shareholders' meeting on August 20, 2026 for dividend vote via absent voting.
17692517.take: Routine capital management disclosure confirming dividend return to shareholders; absent-voting structure indicates administrative efficiency. Non-event unless dividend quantum disappoints relative to prior periods.

17692518.summary: VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited repurchased 50,000 USD Ordinary Shares at GBP 4.480000 per share on 16 July 2026, to be held as treasury shares. Total treasury shares now stand at 6,574,914, with 120,504,609 shares in issue. The total voting rights in the company are 120,504,609 shares following this transaction.
17692518.brief: VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund repurchased 50,000 shares at GBP 4.48 per share on 16 July 2026.
17692518.take: Routine share buyback program execution; modest repurchase size suggests normal capital management rather than significant strategic signal to the market.

17692526.summary: JSC Halyk Bank shareholders will vote by absent ballot on 20 August 2026 on two agenda items: approval of the meeting agenda and adoption of a dividend resolution. The bank proposes paying dividends of KZT 28.09 per common share (ISIN KZ000A0LE0S4) from undistributed net income of previous years for the 2025 period. Payment will commence 7 September 2026 via bank transfer, with record date set for 26 August 2026.
17692526.brief: JSC Halyk Bank to hold EGM on 20 August 2026 to approve dividend payment of KZT 28.09 per share.
17692526.take: Routine dividend announcement supporting shareholder returns; dividend payment indicates available earnings but provides limited market impact as this is standard EGM governance.

17692513.summary: Ninety One plc, the independent investment manager listed on LSE and JSE, disclosed assets under management of £184.0 billion at 30 June 2026. This represents significant growth from £139.7 billion at 30 June 2025 (31.8% year-on-year increase) and £171.8 billion at 31 March 2026 (7.1% quarterly increase). The strong AUM expansion reflects both market appreciation and potential net inflows during the period.
17692513.brief: Ninety One reports Q1 2027 AUM of £184.0bn, up 31.8% year-on-year and 7.1% sequentially.
17692513.take: Solid AUM growth signals positive momentum in client acquisition and market conditions; investors likely to view this favorably as it supports fee-generating revenue, though the announcement lacks detail on net inflows versus market movements.

17692511.summary: SThree plc bought back 28,352 shares on 9 July at an average of 161.14p (ranging 160.2-162.2p) and 36,275 shares on 10 July at 168.04p average (162-171.4p range) via Investec Bank. Total purchase value approximately £10.9m. All shares will be cancelled. Detailed transaction log shows systematic daily purchasing across LSE.
17692511.brief: SThree repurchased 64,627 shares at 160-171p between 9-10 July 2026 for cancellation.
17692511.take: Share buybacks typically signal management confidence in valuation and aim to boost EPS; however, this is routine capital management activity with no immediate operational significance.

17692501.summary: Total Graphite has temporarily suspended production at its Vatomina operation in Madagascar to complete optimization work including drilling, mine planning, and infrastructure upgrades. The company holds US$1.5m unrestricted cash (US$3.8m total with guarantees). An independent technical review identified geological workstreams to improve performance. Drilling is underway with five diamond drill holes completed; results expected Q4 2026. The Montepuez project in Mozambique targets 50,000 tonnes per annum production. Strategic discussions with financial institutions and partners are ongoing.
17692501.brief: Total Graphite pauses Madagascar production for optimization; targets restart December 2026 above 1,000 MT/month.
17692501.take: Production pause signals operational challenges but demonstrates operational learning; market likely views cautiously given cash constraints and delayed restart timeline, though potential strategic partnerships and Mozambique upside offer positive optionality.

17692509.summary: Investor AB's adjusted NAV grew 13% in H1 2026 to SEK 1,214.7bn (SEK 397/share), with Q2 NAV up 9%. Total shareholder return reached 23% H1 (15% Q2), outperforming SIXRX index at 8%. Listed Companies returned 20% H1 (14% Q2), led by ABB's 54.7% return. Patricia Industries returned 1% H1 (-3% Q2) due to valuation multiples compression despite 7% organic sales growth. EQT investments declined 2%. Leverage stood at 1.9% with SEK 28.8bn gross cash.
17692509.brief: Investor AB reports H1 2026 interim results showing strong NAV growth and portfolio outperformance versus index.
17692509.take: Strong NAV growth and outperformance of equity indices suggests effective portfolio management, though Patricia Industries' valuation pressure and Q2 headwinds warrant monitoring. Market likely reads positively overall.

17692691.summary: As of 17 July 2026, BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust plc has 90,423,001 ordinary shares of £0.001 each in issued capital, excluding 27,505,937 shares held in treasury. This figure serves as the denominator for shareholders calculating disclosure obligations under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules (DTR 5.6.1). The announcement is a routine regulatory notification required under Article 15 of the Transparency Directive.
17692691.brief: BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust updates total voting rights denominator to 90.4m shares.
17692691.take: Routine regulatory disclosure updating the share count denominator for disclosure calculations. No material implications for investors; standard compliance housekeeping.

17692694.summary: BlackRock American Income Trust plc disclosed that as of 17 July 2026, it had 61,260,138 issued ordinary shares in circulation, each carrying one vote per share. The company holds 34,101,167 shares in treasury. The 61,260,138 figure serves as the denominator for shareholders calculating whether they must notify their interests under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules provision 5.6.1.
17692694.brief: BlackRock American Income Trust updates voting rights denominator to 61.26m shares as of 17 July 2026.
17692694.take: Routine regulatory disclosure of voting share denominator for transparency compliance; no material implications for share valuation or company fundamentals.

17692696.summary: As at 17 July 2026, BlackRock Energy and Resources Income Trust plc had 100,151,197 issued ordinary shares of £0.01 each in circulation, excluding 35,434,997 shares held in treasury. Shareholders must use the 100.15m figure as the denominator for calculating disclosure thresholds under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules provision 5.6.1 when determining if they are required to notify changes in their shareholding interests.
17692696.brief: BlackRock Energy and Resources Income Trust updates total voting rights denominator to 100.15m shares.
17692696.take: Routine regulatory disclosure updating the share count denominator for disclosure calculations; administrative notification with no material implications for investors or market positioning.

17692697.summary: As of 17 July 2026, BlackRock Smaller Companies Trust plc has 244,753,950 ordinary shares of £0.05 each in issued capital, excluding 53,938,655 shares held in treasury. This figure serves as the denominator for shareholder calculations under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules (DTR 5.6.1) to determine if notification of interests is required.
17692697.brief: BlackRock Smaller Companies Trust discloses total voting rights denominator of 244.75m shares for regulatory compliance.
17692697.take: Routine regulatory disclosure of voting rights denominator required under UK transparency rules; no material implications for investors or share price.

17692706.summary: Eli Lilly announced acquisition of AtaiBeckley Inc., a clinical-stage biotech focused on psychiatric disorder treatments, for approximately $2.8 billion ($6.75 per share) in cash—a 26% premium to closing price on 15 July 2026. Additional contingent value rights of ~$1 billion based on development/regulatory milestones could bring total consideration to $3.8 billion ($9.25 per share), representing 73% premium. AtaiBeckley's lead program BPL-003 has Breakthrough Therapy Designation for treatment-resistant depression. BIOG's 1.1% holding will be reflected in NAV on 16 July. Transaction marks the latest in seven successful M&A exits this year.
17692706.brief: Eli Lilly to acquire BIOG portfolio company AtaiBeckley for $2.8bn ($6.75/share), with $1bn contingent on milestones.
17692706.take: Validates BIOG's small/mid-cap biotech investment strategy and adds to strong year of portfolio realizations; modest 1.1% NAV impact but reinforces conviction narrative for long-term value creation through exits.

17693333.summary: Valterra Platinum's Q2 2026 own-mined PGM production rose 13% to 525,700 ounces, driven by Amandelbult's 116% recovery from 2025 flooding, though offset by declines at Mogalakwena, Mototolo and Unki. Total PGM production increased 1% to 775,400 ounces. However, two work-related fatalities occurred: Thato Makuwa at Mogalakwena (9 June) and Mongezi Mbusi at Amandelbult (11 June). TRIFR rose 10% to 1.41/million hours. 2026 full-year guidance maintained at 3.0-3.4 million ounces M&C production. Realised basket price reached $2,710/oz, up 80% year-on-year.
17693333.brief: Valterra Platinum reports Q2 2026 production up 13% YoY but records two fatal workplace incidents.
17693333.take: Strong operational recovery and elevated commodity prices offset by serious safety failures that triggered TRIFR increase and executive acknowledgment of cultural shortcomings; market likely mixed on operational progress versus safety concerns.

17693334.summary: Georgina Energy PLC granted 9.25 million share awards to directors and officers on 14 July 2026, the first equity incentives since July 2024 IPO. CEO Anthony Hamilton and COO John Heugh each received 2.5m shares; CFO Mark Wallace 2.5m; Chairman Peter Bradley and NED Roy Pitchford 750k each; Company Secretary Silvertree Partners 250k. Awards form part of Long Term Incentive Programme per prospectus. New shares admit to LSE on 22 July 2026, enlarging capital to 222.76m shares.
17693334.brief: Georgina Energy grants 9.25m share awards to management, representing 4.3% of issued capital.
17693334.take: Positive signal: management alignment with shareholder interests through LTIP, though share dilution of 4.3% may concern some investors. Market likely views as standard post-IPO remuneration implementation.

17693350.summary: The Goldman Sachs Group Inc disclosed a holding of 3.019434% in Bodycote PLC as of 14 July 2026, comprising 5,067,477 direct voting rights (2.97%) and 85,251 voting rights via financial instruments (0.05%). This represents a decrease from the previous notification of 3.090482%. The stake was notified on 16 July 2026 and held through multiple Goldman Sachs entities across five ownership chains, primarily via Goldman Sachs Asset Management.
17693350.brief: Goldman Sachs Group reports 3.02% voting stake in Bodycote PLC, down from 3.09%.
17693350.take: Routine major holdings disclosure showing a minor reduction in an institutional investor's stake; no material implications for shareholders or near-term trading.

17693537.summary: Peter Zielke, who served as Director and former Chief Operating Officer for three years, will not seek re-election at the Annual General Meeting on 12 August 2026 and will step down from the Board. Davide Caschili was appointed COO effective 10 June 2026, with Zielke remaining as director to support transition. Following the AGM, Zielke will continue in a consultancy capacity on operational matters. Chairman Larry Lipman credited Zielke's contributions to franchise operations and the Warsaw operational centre of excellence.
17693537.brief: Peter Zielke, former COO, stepping down from board at August AGM; Davide Caschili already appointed as successor in June.
17693537.take: Routine management succession following planned transition; positive signal of orderly handover and continuity planning. No disruption implied given Caschili's June appointment overlap.

17693561.summary: Oxford Instruments purchased 34,500 ordinary shares through J.P. Morgan Securities between 10-16 July 2026 under its announced buyback programme. Daily volumes ranged from 6,600 to 7,000 shares, with volume-weighted average prices between 28.59p and 28.88p. Following cancellation, total issued shares fall to 55,032,742 with no treasury stock held. This reduces share count and increases earnings per share mechanically, supporting capital allocation to existing shareholders.
17693561.brief: Oxford Instruments bought back 34,500 shares during week of 10-16 July 2026 at average prices of 28.6-28.9p per share for cancellation.
17693561.take: Routine capital management action. Share buybacks are mildly positive as they return capital and improve per-share metrics, but this modest weekly tranche suggests measured execution rather than aggressive shareholder-friendly activity.

17693673.summary: Volex plc shareholders can elect to receive the final dividend of 3.2 pence per ordinary share (for year ended 31 March 2026) as new shares instead of cash under the Scrip Dividend Scheme. The reference price will be announced 20 August 2026, with elections required by 28 August 2026 via mandate form, online portal, or CREST. Shareholders with permanent scrip elections need not resubmit forms unless holding shares in CREST.
17693673.brief: Volex offers scrip dividend alternative for 3.2p final dividend, with share reference price to be set 20 August.
17693673.take: Routine disclosure providing shareholders dividend payment flexibility; standard administrative process with no material implications for share price or company fundamentals.

17693672.summary: Swedbank Mortgage AB (wholly owned by Swedbank AB) reported H1 2026 profit of SEK 2,223m versus SEK 3,314m in H1 2025, a 33% decline. Net interest income fell 26% to SEK 3,224m due to margin compression. Total lending reached SEK 1,126bn, with private mortgages at SEK 925bn maintaining 21% market share. Credit quality remains strong with 0.01% impairment ratio and 57% average LTV. Capital position robust at 19.5% total capital ratio. Net financial gains of SEK 122m benefited from derivative revaluation. Funding via SEK 429bn covered bonds outstanding plus SEK 615bn intragroup borrowing.
17693672.brief: Swedbank Mortgage reports H1 2026 profit of SEK 2,223m, down 33% YoY due to lower net interest margins despite volume growth.
17693672.take: Earnings decline driven by structural margin compression rather than credit deterioration or operational issues. Resilient market position and fortress balance sheet offer limited upside but downside protection; likely read as stable hold.

17693671.summary: GCP Infrastructure Investments Ltd purchased 300,000 ordinary shares on 16 July 2026 at a volume-weighted average price of 82.83p, with a range of 81.40p to 83.00p. The buyback was conducted through Canaccord Genuity under shareholder authority granted at the February 2026 AGM. Since the programme's announcement in December 2024, the company has repurchased 79.9 million shares for treasury, reducing voting shares outstanding to 787.9 million.
17693671.brief: GCP Infrastructure repurchased 300,000 shares at 82.83p average price on 16 July 2026.
17693671.take: Routine capital management activity; modest weekly buyback continuing the established programme. Share count reduction typically neutral to slightly positive for EPS accretion in this closed-ended fund context.

17693676.summary: Mid Wynd International Investment Trust is calling a general meeting for 13 August 2026 to seek early renewal of its share buyback authority. The trust has utilised 3,033,500 of 4,542,323 shares (67%) approved at its February GM and expects to fully exhaust this authority before the October AGM. The renewal is necessary to maintain the discount control mechanism (DCM), which targets maintaining share price within ±2% of NAV. The mechanism is a core operational feature enabling smooth capital management.
17693676.brief: Mid Wynd seeks early renewal of share buyback authority ahead of AGM to maintain discount control mechanism.
17693676.take: Routine regulatory disclosure for an investment trust mechanism. Early authority renewal is procedural and operationally sound; buyback activity demonstrates active NAV management. Non-event for investors.

17693678.summary: On 16 July 2026, Artemis UK Future Leaders Plc purchased 4,090 ordinary shares (0.01% of issued capital) at 372.162p per share for treasury. Following the buyback, issued share capital stands at 49,826,436 shares with 20,699,045 held in treasury, leaving 29,127,391 shares with voting rights. The transaction has no material impact on shareholder structure.
17693678.brief: Artemis UK Future Leaders repurchased 4,090 shares at 372p on 16 July, holding them in treasury.
17693678.take: Routine share buyback executed at modest scale; typical capital management activity with minimal shareholder dilution impact. Non-event for markets.

17693690.summary: Trafalgar Capital Management (HK) Limited, via Trafalgar Trading Fund Inc., completely exited its position in Oxford BioDynamics PLC on 15 July 2026. The holding fell from 3.6% of voting rights (comprised of 3.04% direct shareholding and 0.56% through financial instruments) to zero. The disposal was notified to the issuer on 16 July 2026.
17693690.brief: Trafalgar Capital Management divested Oxford BioDynamics holding, reducing stake from 3.6% to zero.
17693690.take: Complete exit by a significant shareholder could signal lack of confidence or portfolio rebalancing; market reaction depends on broader context and whether this was anticipated.

17693686.summary: GCP Asset Backed Income Fund's NAV fell 5.98% to 70.22 pence per share as of 30 June 2026, driven by portfolio revaluations and FX movements of -4.84p, offset partially by 4.22p interest income. Board declares Q2 dividend of 1.58125 pence per share, payable 14 August 2026. Fourth compulsory redemption of at least £45 million at 68.63875 pence per share (NAV less dividend) announced, continuing the company's wind-down strategy which has returned £221.6 million and reduced share capital by 61.11% to date.
17693686.brief: GABI announces NAV decline to 70.22p, Q2 dividend of 1.58p, and fourth £45m+ compulsory redemption.
17693686.take: Declining NAV and continued asset revaluations reflect portfolio challenges during wind-down; fourth redemption maintains capital return momentum but shrinking NAV suggests shareholder value erosion outpacing distributions.

17693703.summary: Tom ap Simon, President of Higher Education and Virtual Learning at Pearson plc, purchased 515.2426 American Depositary Receipts (ADRs) on 14 July 2026 at $11.645 per ADR, totalling $6,000. The purchase was made through Pearson's U.S. Employee Stock Purchase Plan for the offering period 1 January to 30 June 2026, executed on the New York Stock Exchange.
17693703.brief: Pearson President Tom ap Simon purchased 515.2426 ADRs worth $6,000 through employee stock plan.
17693703.take: Routine insider purchase through standard employee share scheme; modest transaction size suggests limited directional signal about management confidence or company outlook.

17693712.summary: GSK's CALM-1 phase III trial met its primary endpoint with camlipixant 50mg twice daily showing statistically significant reductions in 24-hour cough frequency versus placebo at week 12. However, CALM-2 failed to reach statistical significance at week 24 with the same dose, and the 25mg dose failed in both studies. Key secondary endpoints did not meet targets in either trial. GSK concluded the limited efficacy is unlikely to transform patient care and will discontinue RCC development, though the separate BALANCE phase IIb trial for IBS-D and IBS-M continues.
17693712.brief: GSK halts camlipixant development for refractory chronic cough after phase III trials showed insufficient efficacy.
17693712.take: Clinical failure with mixed phase III results and programme termination will likely be viewed negatively by investors as a setback to GSK's pipeline, though the decision to stop development is financially prudent.

17693711.summary: North Atlantic Smaller Companies Investment Trust PLC purchased 50,000 Ordinary Shares at 420 pence per share for cancellation, settling 21 July 2026. Post-cancellation, issued share capital will comprise 125,615,000 Ordinary Shares. The new share count serves as the denominator for shareholders calculating disclosure obligations under FCA Disclosure and Transparency Rules.
17693711.brief: NASCIT bought back and cancelled 50,000 shares at 420p per share on 17 July 2026.
17693711.take: Share buyback with cancellation reduces share count and potentially increases EPS; neutral to modestly positive signal on capital allocation, though modest scale suggests routine treasury management.

17693707.summary: BH Macro Limited purchased 29,372 of its ordinary Sterling shares on 16 July 2026 via J.P. Morgan Securities plc at a weighted average price of 4.22p. The shares will be held in treasury. Following this transaction, total voting rights in the company stand at 459,458,929, with 300,152,775 Sterling shares in issue (excluding treasury) and 77,602,937 held in treasury.
17693707.brief: BH Macro repurchased 29,372 Sterling shares at 4.22p on 16 July, holding them in treasury.
17693707.take: Routine share buyback disclosed for regulatory compliance; modest purchase size suggests capital management rather than significant shareholder value signaling. Non-event for most investors.

17693721.summary: Johnson Matthey has completed the sale of its Catalyst Technologies business to Honeywell for £1.325 billion enterprise value on a cash and debt-free basis.
